tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria with low urine specific gravity, indicating possible nephrotic syndrome or glomerular pathology. Urine is abnormal in appearance and color, with elevated bilirubin, but no evidence of infection. HbA1c is low, ruling out chronic hyperglycemia. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
